parsed.expected_effect: The rule will now notify the agent when they attempt to commit changes that include modifications to src/functions/ (e.g., adding archive.ts) without having modified src/types.ts in the same session, covering the opaque fixture, script visible, and direct visible violation traces.
parsed.policy_yaml:
# directive: "When adding new audit operations: 1. `src/types.ts` -- add to
#   AuditEntry.operation union type"
# repo: "rohitg00__agentmemory"
# statement_id: 6
# enforceability: "cross_event"
# context_required: "project"
# notes: "The directive requires updating src/types.ts when adding new audit
#   operations. The original rule failed to catch violations where the agent
#   wrote audit files and committed them without updating types.ts. This revision
#   broadens the trigger to include any write to src/functions/ (covering direct
#   writes and script-generated files) and fires a notification on commit if
#   src/types.ts has not been modified in the same session. This approximates the
#   dependency by ensuring the agent is reminded to update types.ts before
#   committing changes to audit functions."
version: 1
policy: |
  source AGENT = exec "claude"
  source AUDIT_WRITE = file "src/functions/**"
  source TYPES_UPDATE = file "src/types.ts"

  rule update-types-for-audit-ops:
    notify exec "git" "commit" if AGENT and AUDIT_WRITE and not TYPES_UPDATE
    because "When adding new audit operations, you must also update src/types.ts to add the operation to the AuditEntry.operation union type"
parsed.rationale: The original rule relied on a generic 'AUDIT_CHANGE' source that likely failed to propagate correctly in opaque fixture scenarios or script-based workflows. By explicitly defining AUDIT_WRITE as writes to src/functions/** and adding a negative condition 'not TYPES_UPDATE', we ensure the rule triggers when audit files are modified but the corresponding type definitions are not. This covers direct writes, script-generated files, and ensures the notification fires at commit time if the dependency is missing.
